content.explanation: GET reads data and has no side effects — you can call it a thousand times and nothing on the server changes. POST is different: it's how you submit or create data, sending a payload (usually JSON) in the request body that the server acts on.

check.statement: Fill in the blank: to create a new device record via the API, you'd send an HTTP ____ request with the device data in the request body, not a GET.

solution.explanation: POST is the standard HTTP method for creating/submitting data, carried in the request body — GET is for reading only and conventionally carries no body, which is why it's the wrong tool for creating a new record.
